metadata.summary: A collection of useful Python Classes, Functions, and Constants

documentation:
# Hephaestus

## Table of Contents
1. [Objective](#objective)
2. [Use](#use)
    - [Installation](#installation)
    - [Testing](#testing)
    - [Generating Documentation](#generating-documentation)
3. [Inspirations](#inspirations)
4. [Future Plans](#future-plans)
    - [Short Term Goals](#short-term-goals)
    - [Long Term Goals](#long-term-goals)


## Objective

The objective of this project was/is to stop repeating myself. I've gotten tired of rewriting the same modules and utilities
for various professional and hobby project.

Started in December 2024, well... technically, since about 2022.

## Use

Project is free for use and uploaded to [PyPi under the name hephaestus-lib](https://pypi.org/project/hephaestus-lib/).

Unfortunately, someone already took the name Hephaestus on PyPi so...

All modules are still referenced like so:

```
# myfile.py

from hephaestus.common.colors import Colors
from hephaestus.testing.pytest.fixtures import *

```

While not intentionally developed to be cross-platform, most of the stuff in here is. It just didn't cost that that much more effort 
or brainpower to avoid.

This library is not intended to be used as a template or guide, but it can definitely can be used as "inspiration." Please link back to this repo or [MalakaiSpann.com](https://malakaispann.com) if you do.

### Installation

```bash
pip install hephaestus-lib
```

### Testing

```bash
scripts/run_pytest
```

### Generating Documentation
```bash
scripts/generate_documentation
```

Friendly reminder: don't be a weirdo who steals source code. Even when credited, it's still pretty shady to straight copy someone else's work. The occasional peak when you're stuck is fine, but learn how to do it yourself... it'll get you much farther!

## Future Plans

This is likely going to be a Work-In-Progress for quite some time. There's lots to do even with the small amounts of 
code already in here.

I plan on updating/improving this library as long as I use Python 🙂.  

### Short Term Goals
- Get sane CI pipeline together.
    - A lot of the scripts are already there, just kinda need to set everything up to talk to one another.
- Finish documentation. Get it together man.
- Implement code coverage reporting. This is always a good step
- Knock out some unit and functional tests. Many of the things in here are 

### Long Term Goals
- World Peace
- Afford a house
- Live in Japan

Feel free to suggest improvements or get down and dirty (with protection of course... a merge request – I mean a merge request).

Thanks, 

\- Kay

classes.name: hephaestus.io.stream.NullStreamer
documentation:
A streaming interface that does not output.

This class should only be used where a FileIO object is necessary, but output is not wanted.

classes.name: hephaestus.io.stream.LogStreamer

methods.name: hephaestus.io.stream.LogStreamer.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    logger: the logger object to stream output to.
    log_level: the default logging level to stream output at. Defaults to logging.INFO.

methods.name: hephaestus.io.stream.LogStreamer.write
documentation:
Logs passed information at specified level.

log_level: the logging level to stream output at. Defaults the object's log_level.

methods.name: hephaestus.io.stream.LogStreamer.flush
documentation: Flushes the handlers for each stream available to the logger.

methods.name: hephaestus.io.stream.LogStreamer.isatty
documentation:
Returns whether the logger has an output stream available.

Returns:
    True if the logger has at least one handler; False otherwise.
A streaming interface that enables write operations directly to logger object.

classes.name: hephaestus.patterns.singleton.Singleton

methods.name: hephaestus.patterns.singleton.Singleton.__call__
documentation: Initializes or returns available singleton objects.

A Pythonic, thread-safe implementation of the Singleton pattern.

This class is intended to be used as the meta class for another class.
i.e.  class MyClass(metaclass=Singleton):
        ...

Each Singleton object will have access to a standard library thread mutex via `self._lock` for basic thread safety.
It is the responsibility of the subclass implementer to ensure operations are atomic.

Final Warnings:
    - If overriding `__call__`, be sure to reference the `__call__` method as implemented in this class.
    - Do not modify `__shared_instances`.

functions.name: hephaestus.io.file.validate_path
documentation:
Resolves a path and verifies it exists.

Args:
    path: the path to validate.

Returns:
    A path object resolved to be absolute.

Raises:
    FileError: if the path cannot be found.

functions.name: hephaestus.io.file.create_directory
documentation:
Attempts to create directory at specified location.

Args:
    folder: the path to the directory.

Returns:
    A Path object with the path to the directory.

Raises:
    FileError if directory creation failed.

variables.name: hephaestus.io.file._logger
documentation: PathLike: A string, Path, or similar representation of a file path.

classes.name: hephaestus.io.file.FileError
documentation: Indicates an error has occurred while attempting a file operation.

classes.name: hephaestus.common.exceptions.LoggedException
documentation:
An exception whose message is logged at the error level.

This class is meant to be used as the base class for any other custom
exceptions. It logs the error message for later viewing.

functions.name: hephaestus.io.subprocess._exec
documentation:
Executes a command, logging results as specified.

Args:
    cmd: the command to run.
    enable_output: whether to log captured output.
    log_level: the level to log cmd output at. Ignored if enable_output set to False. Defaults to DEBUG.

Returns:
    The output of the cmd as captured line-by-line. If the command was unsuccessful, None will be returned.

Notes:
    This function overwrites various commonly set arguments to subprocess.run/subprocess.popen including
    `stdout`, `stderr`, and `universal_newlines`.

    Users should only expect `enable_output` to change the behavior of what's actually output.
TODO: option to pass the information of the actual caller

functions.name: hephaestus.io.subprocess.command_successful
documentation:
Checks if command returned 'Success' status.

Args:
    cmd: the command to run.

Note:
    This function doesn't capture or return any command output.
    It's intended to be used in pass/fail-type scenarios involving subprocesses.

functions.name: hephaestus.io.subprocess.run_command
documentation:
Runs command and logs output as specified.

Args:
    cmd: the command to run.
    err: the error to display if the command fails.
    cleanup: the function to run in the event of a failure.
    enable_output: whether to log captured output. Defaults to True.
    log_level: the level to log cmd output at. Ignored if enable_output set to False. Defaults to DEBUG.

Raises:
    SubprocessError if the command fails for any reason.

Notes:
    This function overwrites various commonly set arguments to subprocess.run/subprocess.popen including
    `stdout`, `stderr`, and `universal_newlines`.

    Users should only expect `enable_output` to change the behavior of what's actually output.

classes.name: hephaestus.io.subprocess.SubprocessError
documentation: Indicates an unexpected error has occurred while attempting a subprocess operation.
Public

functions.name: hephaestus.testing.pytest.fixtures.env.reset_env
documentation: Resets Hephaestus memory and such after each test.

functions.name: hephaestus.testing.pytest.fixtures.logging.module_logger
documentation:
Creates a logger for every test module.

Args:
    request: the request fixture that gives access to the calling module.

Yields:
    a logger configured with the name of the test module.

functions.name: hephaestus.testing.pytest.fixtures.logging.logger
documentation:
Provides the module's logger.

Args:
    module_logger: the logger generated for the module.

Yields:
    A ready-to-use logger object.

functions.name: hephaestus.util.logging._create_log_folder
documentation:
Attempts to create the parent of the log file.

Args:
    log_file: the file where logs will be stored to.

Returns:
    True if the the parent directory of log_file exists. False otherwise.

Note:
    There is no guarantee that an existing directory will enable the program to write
    a file to the directory at this time.

functions.name: hephaestus.util.logging.configure_root_logger
documentation:
Configures logger that ever other logger propagates up to.

Args:
    min_level: the minimum log level to pipe to stdout. Defaults to logging.INFO.
    log_file: the absolute path to the log file to generate. Defaults to None.
    enable_color: whether output to stdout should be colored. Defaults to LogFormatter.DEFAULT_ENABLE_COLOR.
    time_expr: a method that converts the seconds since the epoch to a time.struct_time
        object. Defaults to LogFormatter.DEFAULT_TIME_EXPR.
    fmt_opts: a mapping of the format options to use for each level. Defaults to LogFormatter.DEFAULT_FMT_OPTS.

Note:
    If log_file is provided, a file will attempt to be generated. Logs saved to file will never
    contain colored output.

functions.name: hephaestus.util.logging.get_logger
documentation:
Creates a log of application activity.

Args:
    name: the name of the calling module. Defaults to None.
    root: the path to the root of the project. Defaults to none.
    file_path: the path to the file where this logger will be used. Only use this
    parameter when fine control over logger naming is necessary.

Returns:
    A bare logger object that accepts all default levels of log messages.

Note:
    If the calling file is not in the project and the `name` arg is provided,
    this acts just like logging.getLogger(name). If the file is in the project AND
    the `root` arg is provided, the name will be relative to the root of the project.
    If `file_path` is specified, it will be used to name the logger instead of the calling
    file.

classes.name: hephaestus.util.logging.FormatOptions
documentation:
Format Options for a logging.Formatter.

Args:
    fmt: the format string to use
    default_color: the ANSI color code used to color output.
    style: the 'format' style to use. '{', '%'

classes.name: hephaestus.util.logging.LogFormatter

methods.name: hephaestus.util.logging.LogFormatter._create_formatter
documentation:
Creates log formatters from string and time expression method.

Args:
    fmt: the template for the formatter as defined in logging docs.
    style: the 'format' style to use. '{', '%'.

Returns:
    A ready-to-go formatting object.

methods.name: hephaestus.util.logging.LogFormatter._construct_formatters
documentation:
Creates formatter objects.

Args:
    fmt_opts: a mapping of the format options to use for each level.

Returns:
    A formatter object for each log level with the specified template.

methods.name: hephaestus.util.logging.LogFormatter.format
documentation:
Converts log record into customized format.

Args:
    record: logging object (attributes + message).

Returns:
    A formatted string.

Defines common message format for files.

Args:
    enable_color: whether formatter should include ASCII-based coloring.
    time_expr: the method to convert seconds since epoch to a time.struct_time object.
    fmt_opts: a mapping of the format options to use for each level.
Formatting

functions.name: hephaestus.decorators.track.track
documentation:
Records function call for later examination.

Args:
    to_track : the function to track.

Returns:
    The passed function with minor modification pre and post-call
    to support tracking capability.

Note:
    Can be used as a decorator:

    @track
    def print_copy(*args):
        ...

    Or like a regular function:

    print_copy = track(to_track=print_copy)

classes.name: hephaestus.decorators.track.Trace_Queue

methods.name: hephaestus.decorators.track.Trace_Queue.get
documentation:
_summary_

Returns:
    _description_
An object capable of storing

functions.name: hephaestus.decorators.reference.__return_none
documentation:
Empty function used to always get None on call.

Returns:
    None

functions.name: hephaestus.decorators.reference.__method_wrapper
documentation:
Wraps methods, ensuring calls go to object stored by reference.

Args:
    getter: the function to use a "getter" for the stored object.
    if_none: the function to use should the stored object be Null.
    method_name: the name of method to wrap.

Returns:
    A callable function that acts as a proxy for the method to wrap.

Note:
    Any args passed to the wrapped method will be passed to the stored object's
    actual method without modification.

functions.name: hephaestus.decorators.reference.reference_ignore
documentation:
Specifies to avoid proxying the method call using the store object.

Args:
    method: the method to avoid wrapping.

Raises:
    ReferenceError: if another reference modifier has already been specified
    for the method.

Returns:
    The method, largely unmodified.

functions.name: hephaestus.decorators.reference.reference_getter
documentation:
Specifies to use the method to get the stored object.

Args:
    method: the method that returns the stored object.

Raises:
    ReferenceError: if another reference modifier has already been specified
    for the method.

Returns:
    The method, largely unmodified.

functions.name: hephaestus.decorators.reference.reference
documentation:
Specifies the class holds a stored object.

Args:
    cls: the class definition to wrap.

Raises:
    ReferenceError: should the passed object not be a class definition.

Returns:
    The class definition with some minor tweaks to instantiation and object creation
    logic.

Note:
    Generally, any method defined for the class other than the "getter" should
    be annotated with @reference_ignore.

classes.name: hephaestus.decorators.reference.__ReferenceMap
documentation: Private

classes.name: hephaestus.decorators.reference.ReferenceError
documentation: Public

functions.name: hephaestus.testing.swte.large_banner
documentation:
Logs message between dividers.

```
param msg

a string containing the desired text.
```

Note:

: Output will look like '...============================ \<msg> =============================...'
  Intended to be used with single-line messages only. Multi-line will look strange :).
Logging Functions

functions.name: hephaestus.testing.swte.small_banner
documentation:
Logs message in between some dashes.

```
param msg

a string containing the desired text.
```

Note:

: Output will look like:
  '
  ----------------...
  \<line 1>
  \<line 2>
  ...
  \<line n>
  ----------------...
  '
  Safe for use with multi-line messages.

functions.name: hephaestus.testing.pytest.configure._get_failure_repr
documentation:
Extracts test's failure reason if available.

```
param report

the Test report for a given phase of testing.

return

the string representation of the cause of test failure if available. If not, an empty string.
```
Utility

functions.name: hephaestus.testing.pytest.configure.pytest_configure
documentation:
Disable the terminal entirely.

Note:
    In order to customize the output to terminal even a little, you'd have to
    rewrite about 250+ lines of incredibly obtuse internal PyTest code.

    If not doing this, you either have to deal with uncolored results (which are somewhat
    difficult to parse at a glance) or ANSI codes showing up in the log file (also difficult to parse).

    Instead we'll just use various public hooks to log the results of each test.

functions.name: hephaestus.testing.pytest.configure.pytest_runtest_makereport
documentation:
Logs each function's docstring.

Note:
    This is done here instead of in the fixture because the function level fixtures don't run when a function is marked to be skipped.
    We always want to know what would be run and why it wasn't.

functions.name: hephaestus.testing.pytest.configure.pytest_exception_interact
documentation: Logs failure/exception message as it's thrown.

functions.name: hephaestus.testing.pytest.configure.pytest_report_teststatus
documentation: Logs and saves the result of the test.

functions.name: hephaestus.testing.pytest.configure.pytest_unconfigure
documentation: Display test stats and summary before exiting.

functions.name: hephaestus.testing.pytest.configure.pytest_sessionfinish
documentation: Returns the status of the program based on test failures.
